title: TAS
description: Topology Agnostic Structure (TAS) v2 — the minimum simulatable power-converter circuit (the SPICE analogy is a complete deck: CIAS bricks instantiated as stages, wired at their terminals, with stimulus and analyses). Four sections: inputs (the spec it must satisfy), topology (the Lego assembly of CIAS bricks into stages), outputs (how it performs), simulation (simulator-agnostic stimulus + analyses). A full product converter would add more CIAS bricks (protection, aux, housekeeping) at a converter level above TAS — out of scope here.

inputs.description: Design specification + operating points the converter must satisfy.

topology.description: The Lego assembly: stages (each instantiating one CIAS brick) and the inter-stage connections that wire them.

outputs.description: Computed/measured results: per-design metrics plus per-operating-point losses, stresses, and per-stage breakdowns.

simulation.description: Simulator-agnostic analysis setup: which analyses to run, model-library entries, and per-component model bindings/overrides keyed by CIAS component name. Translatable to SPICE, PLECS, Modelica, etc.
Simulator-agnostic simulation setup. Carries WHAT to analyse and WHICH models to bind — never simulator-specific command syntax.

analyses.description: Analyses to run on the converter.

models.description: Model-library entries referenced by component bindings. Each is tagged with its format so any simulator backend can select the entries it understands.

overrides.description: Per-component model bindings / parameter overrides, keyed stage-qualified ({stage, component}). Used when a part's simulation behaviour cannot be inferred from its PEAS data.

stimulus.description: Open-loop drive: directly imposes a waveform on a switch/driver (its gate or enable), bypassing any controlStage. Lets the same TAS run open-loop (stimulus) or closed-loop (controlStage) — the two may coexist; a stimulus on a switch takes precedence over a drive for that switch.

initialConditions.description: Initial transient state: nodes that begin pre-charged to a given voltage at t=0 rather than being solved from a cold DC operating point. Simulator-agnostic — a SPICE backend realises these as .ic plus a use-initial-conditions transient; other backends as initial-state assignments. Needed by converters whose steady state is unreachable from a cold start: e.g. resonant tanks whose series capacitors leave the DC operating point ill-conditioned, or active synchronous rectifiers that cannot self-start into a 0 V output.

initialCondition.title: initialCondition
initialCondition.description: One node's initial voltage at t=0.

node.description: Node to initialise — an inter-stage connection name or external-port name (e.g. the output port).

voltage.description: Initial node voltage at t=0, in volts.

stimulus.title: stimulus
stimulus.description: One open-loop drive applied to a switch/driver inside a stage's CIAS brick.

stage.description: Stage instance whose brick contains the driven component.

component.description: component.name within that stage's brick (a switch or driver).

waveform.title: waveform
waveform.$comment: Simulator-agnostic drive waveform. Closed variants discriminated by type; a translator maps each to its simulator's source primitive (e.g. pwm → a pulse V-source, constant → a DC level).

phase.description: Phase offset, in degrees (e.g. 180 for the complementary leg, or for interleaving).

deadTime.description: Dead time before turn-on, in seconds.

constantWaveform.title: constantWaveform
constantWaveform.description: A constant logic/DC level (e.g. tie an enable high).

value.description: Constant level (logic 0/1, or a DC voltage).

analysis.title: analysis
analysis.$comment: Simulator-agnostic analysis request. One of four closed variants discriminated by type. A translator maps each to its simulator's native directive (e.g. transient → SPICE .tran, PLECS Transient block).

transientAnalysis.title: transientAnalysis
transientAnalysis.description: Time-domain analysis over a fixed window.

startTime.description: Start of the recorded window, in seconds (default 0).

maximumTimeStep.description: Maximum solver time step, in seconds.

pointsPerInterval.description: Points per decade/octave, or total points for a linear sweep.

dcSweepAnalysis.title: dcSweepAnalysis
dcSweepAnalysis.description: Sweep a DC source value and record the operating point at each step.

sweptComponent.description: Name of the component (CIAS topology) whose source value is swept (e.g. an input voltage/current source).

start.description: Sweep start value, in the source's SI unit (V or A).

stop.description: Sweep stop value, in the source's SI unit.

step.description: Sweep increment, in the source's SI unit.

operatingPointAnalysis.title: operatingPointAnalysis
operatingPointAnalysis.description: Solve the steady-state DC operating point only.

simulationModel.title: simulationModel
simulationModel.description: One model-library entry. The format tag lets a translator select the entries it understands; the same logical model may appear once per format.

format.description: Format/dialect of the definition payload, so a simulator backend can pick the entries it understands.

definition.description: The model text in the given format, or a URI to a model file.

componentModel.title: componentModel
componentModel.description: Binds a model and/or parameter overrides to one component inside a stage's CIAS brick.

stage.description: Stage instance whose brick contains the component.

component.description: component.name within that stage's brick this binding applies to.

model.description: Name of an entry in simulation.models[] (or an external library model) to bind to this component.

parameters.description: Agnostic name/value parameter overrides applied to the bound model.

value.description: Parameter value (numeric, or a string expression / engineering-notation literal).
